# google_chronicle_feed

The FeedsService is responsible for configuring and managing the ingestion of third-party security data and logs into Google Security Operations through various feed creation, updates, and lifecycle management, and schema validation.

~> **Warning:** This resource is in beta, and should be used with the terraform-provider-google-beta provider.
See [Provider Versions](../guides/provider_versions.html.markdown) for more details on beta resources.

To get more information about Feed, see:

* [API documentation](https://docs.cloud.google.com/chronicle/docs/reference/rest/v1beta/projects.locations.instances.feeds)
* How-to Guides
    * [Feed management overview](https://docs.cloud.google.com/chronicle/docs/administration/feed-management-overview)

## Example Usage - Chronicle Feed Basic


```hcl
# This is a basic example for Terraform support in Chronicle feeds APIs.

resource "google_chronicle_feed" "example_feed" {
  location     = "us"
  provider     = google-beta
  instance     = "00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000"
  display_name = "test-feeds"

  details {
    feed_source_type = "HTTPS_PUSH_GOOGLE_CLOUD_PUBSUB"
    log_type         = "projects/my-project-name/locations/us/instances/00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000/logTypes/GCP_CLOUDAUDIT"
    https_push_google_cloud_pubsub_settings {
      split_delimiter = "\n"
    }
  }
}
```
## Example Usage - Chronicle Feed Gcs


```hcl
# This is an example of a Chronicle feed using the pre_create hook to fetch the service account.
# Valid for source type: GOOGLE_CLOUD_STORAGE_EVENT_DRIVEN and GOOGLE_CLOUD_STORAGE.

# 0. Get the project's GCS service account (for Storage Notifications)
data "google_storage_project_service_account" "gcs_account" {
  provider = google-beta
  project  = "my-project-name"
}

# 1. Create the Pub/Sub Topic
resource "google_pubsub_topic" "test_topic" {
  provider = google-beta
  name     = "chronicle-test-topic-my-project-name-%{random_suffix}"
  project  = "my-project-name"
}

# 2. Grant GCS permission to publish to the topic
# This must remain so GCS can send file notifications to Pub/Sub
resource "google_pubsub_topic_iam_member" "gcs_publisher" {
  provider = google-beta
  topic    = google_pubsub_topic.test_topic.name
  role     = "roles/pubsub.publisher"
  member   = "serviceAccount:${data.google_storage_project_service_account.gcs_account.email_address}"
}

# 3. Create the Pub/Sub Subscription
resource "google_pubsub_subscription" "test_subscription" {
  provider = google-beta
  name     = "chronicle-test-sub-my-project-name-%{random_suffix}"
  project  = "my-project-name"
  topic    = google_pubsub_topic.test_topic.name

  message_retention_duration = "1200s"
}

# 4. Define the GCS Bucket
resource "google_storage_bucket" "test_bucket" {
  provider      = google-beta
  name          = "chronicle-test-bucket-my-project-name-%{random_suffix}"
  project       = "my-project-name"
  location      = "US"
  force_destroy = true
}

# 5. Link GCS bucket to Pub/Sub Topic
resource "google_storage_notification" "notification" {
  provider       = google-beta
  bucket         = google_storage_bucket.test_bucket.name
  payload_format = "JSON_API_V1"
  topic          = google_pubsub_topic.test_topic.id
  event_types    = ["OBJECT_FINALIZE"]

  depends_on = [google_pubsub_topic_iam_member.gcs_publisher]
}

# 6. The Chronicle Feed Resource
# The required permissions (Storage Admin and Pub/Sub Subscriber) are assumed to be
# granted manually at the project level prior to running this test.
resource "google_chronicle_feed" "example_feed" {
  provider     = google-beta
  location     = "us"
  instance     = "00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000"
  display_name = "test-feeds"

  details {
    feed_source_type = "GOOGLE_CLOUD_STORAGE_EVENT_DRIVEN"
    log_type         = "projects/my-project-name/locations/us/instances/00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000/logTypes/GCP_CLOUDAUDIT"

    google_cloud_storage_event_driven_settings {
        bucket_uri          = "gs://${google_storage_bucket.test_bucket.name}"
        pubsub_subscription = "projects/my-project-name/subscriptions/${google_pubsub_subscription.test_subscription.name}"
        max_lookback_days   = 90
    }
  }

  depends_on = [google_storage_notification.notification]
}

# 7. Output the dynamically fetched service account to prove the pre_create hook worked
output "fetched_feed_service_account" {
  value = google_chronicle_feed.example_feed.feed_service_account
}
```
## Example Usage - Chronicle Feed Https Push


```hcl
# This is an example of a Chronicle feed using the post_create hook to fetch the secret.
# Valid for source type: HTTPS_PUSH_AMAZON_KINESIS_FIREHOSE.

resource "google_chronicle_feed" "example_feed" {
  provider     = google-beta
  location     = "us"
  instance     = "00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000"
  display_name = "test-feeds"

  details {
    feed_source_type = "HTTPS_PUSH_AMAZON_KINESIS_FIREHOSE"
    log_type         = "projects/my-project-name/locations/us/instances/00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000/logTypes/GCP_CLOUDAUDIT"
    https_push_amazon_kinesis_firehose_settings {
        split_delimiter = "\n"
    }
  }
}

output "feed_secret" {
  value     = google_chronicle_feed.example_feed.secret
  sensitive = true
}
```

## Argument Reference

The following arguments are supported:


* `location` -
  (Required)
  Resource ID segment making up resource `name`. It identifies the resource within its parent collection as described in https://google.aip.dev/122.

* `instance` -
  (Required)
  Resource ID segment making up resource `name`. It identifies the resource within its parent collection as described in https://google.aip.dev/122.


* `enabled` -
  (Optional)
  Whether the feed is enabled.

* `details` -
  (Optional)
  Additional details of the feed, these details are dynamic and will be
  different for each of the feeds.
  Structure is [documented below](#nested_details).

* `display_name` -
  (Optional)
  Customer-provided feed name.

* `failure_details` -
  (Optional)
  FeedFailureDetails contains details about the errors thrown by chronicle for
  the feeds. These are user visible details. These details help user identify
  the root cause and take appropriate action for the feed errors.
  NEXT TAG: 5
  Structure is [documented below](#nested_failure_details).

* `feed` -
  (Optional)
  Resource ID segment making up resource `name`. It identifies the resource within its parent collection as described in https://google.aip.dev/122.

* `project` - (Optional) The ID of the project in which the resource belongs.
    If it is not provided, the provider project is used.

* `deletion_policy` - (Optional) Whether Terraform will be prevented from destroying the resource. Defaults to DELETE.
	When a 'terraform destroy' or 'terraform apply' would delete the resource,
	the command will fail if this field is set to "PREVENT" in Terraform state.
	When set to "ABANDON", the command will remove the resource from Terraform
	management without updating or deleting the resource in the API.
	When set to "DELETE", deleting the resource is allowed.
* `secret` - (Optional) Output only. The secret generated for the feed. This is only available when the feed source type is HTTPS_PUSH_AMAZON_KINESIS_FIREHOSE.

* `feed_service_account` - (Optional) Output only. The service account used by Chronicle to ingest data from Cloud Storage. This is only available when the feed source type is GOOGLE_CLOUD_STORAGE_EVENT_DRIVEN or GOOGLE_CLOUD_STORAGE.



<a name="nested_details"></a>The `details` block supports:

* `amazon_kinesis_firehose_settings` -
  (Optional)
  Settings required by Amazon Kinesis Firehose Feeds(HTTP-Push).

* `amazon_s3_settings` -
  (Optional)
  Amazon S3 settings.
  Structure is [documented below](#nested_details_amazon_s3_settings).

* `amazon_s3_v2_settings` -

<a name="nested_details_amazon_s3_settings"></a>The `amazon_s3_settings` block supports:

* `authentication` -
  (Optional)
  Amazon S3 auth.
  Structure is [documented below](#nested_details_amazon_s3_settings_authentication).

* `s3_uri` -
  (Required)
  S3 URI.

* `source_deletion_option` -
  (Required)
  Possible values:
  SOURCE_DELETION_NEVER
  SOURCE_DELETION_ON_SUCCESS
  SOURCE_DELETION_ON_SUCCESS_FILES_ONLY

* `source_type` -
  (Required)
  Possible values:
  FILES
  FOLDERS
  FOLDERS_RECURSIVE


<a name="nested_details_amazon_s3_settings_authentication"></a>The `authentication` block supports:

* `access_key_id` -
  (Optional)
  Access key ID. Used when using access key auth.

* `client_id` -
  (Optional)
  Client ID. Used when using OAuth auth.

* `client_secret` -
  (Optional)
  Client secret. Used when using OAuth auth.

* `refresh_uri` -
  (Optional)
  Refresh URI. Used when using OAuth auth.
  **Note**: This property is sensitive and will not be displayed in the plan.

* `region` -
  (Required)
  Possible values:
  US_EAST_1
  US_EAST_2
  US_WEST_1
  US_WEST_2
  US_GOV_CLOUD
  US_GOV_EAST_1
  EU_WEST_1
  EU_WEST_2
  EU_WEST_3
  EU_CENTRAL_1
  EU_NORTH_1
  EU_SOUTH_1
  AP_SOUTH_1
  AP_SOUTHEAST_1
  AP_SOUTHEAST_2
  AP_SOUTHEAST_3
  AP_NORTHEAST_1
  AP_NORTHEAST_2
  AP_NORTHEAST_3
  AP_EAST_1
  SA_EAST_1
  CN_NORTH_1
  CN_NORTHWEST_1
  CA_CENTRAL_1
  AF_SOUTH_1
  ME_SOUTH_1
  AP_SOUTH_2
  AP_SOUTHEAST_4
  CA_WEST_1
  EU_SOUTH_2
  EU_CENTRAL_2
  IL_CENTRAL_1
  ME_CENTRAL_1

* `secret_access_key` -
  (Optional)
  Secret access key. Used when using access key auth.

<a name="nested_details_amazon_s3_v2_settings"></a>The `amazon_s3_v2_settings` block supports:

* `authentication` -
  (Required)
  A message containing fields used to authenticate with Amazon S3.
  Structure is [documented below](#nested_details_amazon_s3_v2_settings_authentication).

* `chronicle_service_account` -
  (Output)
  SA that will read data, this is Storage Transfer Service SA of Customer's
  Tenancy Project.

* `max_lookback_days` -
  (Optional)
  Maximum File Age to ingest in days.

* `s3_uri` -
  (Required)
  S3 URI.

* `source_deletion_option` -
  (Optional)
  Possible values:
  NEVER
  ON_SUCCESS

<a name="nested_details_amazon_s3_v2_settings_authentication_access_key_secret_auth"></a>The `access_key_secret_auth` block supports:

* `access_key_id` -
  (Required)

* `secret_access_key` -
  (Required)

  **Note**: This property is sensitive and will not be displayed in the plan.

  Info for username and secret based authentication.

<a name="nested_details_fox_it_stix_settings_authentication"></a>The `authentication` block supports:

* `secret` -
  (Optional)

  **Note**: This property is sensitive and will not be displayed in the plan.

* `user` -
  (Optional)
  Username of an identity used for authentication.

<a name="nested_details_fox_it_stix_settings_ssl"></a>The `ssl` block supports:

* `encoded_private_key` -
  (Optional)
  The encoded private key. The string should be a private key in PEM format,
  and should include the begin header and end footer lines. It may also
  include newlines.
  Example:
  -----BEGIN RSA PRIVATE KEY-----
  Proc-Type: 4,ENCRYPTED
  DEK-Info: DES-EDE3-CBC,F23074E02CF47304
  
  -----END RSA PRIVATE KEY-----
  **Note**: This property is sensitive and will not be displayed in the plan.

* `ssl_certificate` -
  (Optional)
  The encoded SSL certificate. The string should be an SSL certificate in
  PEM format, and should include the begin header and end footer lines. It
  may also include newlines.
  Example:
  -----BEGIN CERTIFICATE-----
  -----END CERTIFICATE-----
  **Note**: This property is sensitive and will not be displayed in the plan.

<a name="nested_details_gcs_settings"></a>The `gcs_settings` block supports:

* `bucket_uri` -
  (Optional)

  SOURCE_DELETION_ON_SUCCESS

* `source_deletion_option` -
  (Optional)

<a name="nested_details_rh_isac_ioc_settings_authentication"></a>The `authentication` block supports:

* `client_id` -
  (Optional)
  Client ID.

* `client_secret` -
  (Optional)
  Client secret.
  **Note**: This property is sensitive and will not be displayed in the plan.

* `token_endpoint` -
  (Optional)
  Token endpoint.

<a name="nested_details_salesforce_settings"></a>The `salesforce_settings` block supports:

* `hostname` -
  (Optional)
  API hostname.

* `oauth_jwt_credentials` -
  (Optional)
  OAuth 2.0 JWT grant. See, https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7519
  Structure is [documented below](#nested_details_salesforce_settings_oauth_jwt_credentials).

* `oauth_password_grant_auth` -
  (Optional)
  OAuth 2.0 password grant. See https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc6749.
  Structure is [documented below](#nested_details_salesforce_settings_oauth_password_grant_auth).


<a name="nested_details_salesforce_settings_oauth_jwt_credentials"></a>The `oauth_jwt_credentials` block supports:

* `claims` -
  (Optional)
  Claims identifying a specific customer.
  Structure is [documented below](#nested_details_salesforce_settings_oauth_jwt_credentials_claims).

* `rs_credentials` -
  (Optional)
  RS credentials.
  Structure is [documented below](#nested_details_salesforce_settings_oauth_jwt_credentials_rs_credentials).

* `token_endpoint` -
  (Optional)
  Token endpoint to get the OAuth token from.


<a name="nested_details_salesforce_settings_oauth_jwt_credentials_claims"></a>The `claims` block supports:

* `audience` -
  (Optional)
  Audience.

* `issuer` -
  (Optional)
  Issuer. Usually the client_id.

* `subject` -
  (Optional)
  Subject. Usually the email.

<a name="nested_details_salesforce_settings_oauth_jwt_credentials_rs_credentials"></a>The `rs_credentials` block supports:

* `private_key` -
  (Optional)
  Private key in PEM format.
  **Note**: This property is sensitive and will not be displayed in the plan.

<a name="nested_details_salesforce_settings_oauth_password_grant_auth"></a>The `oauth_password_grant_auth` block supports:

  (Optional)
  Client ID.

* `client_secret` -
  (Optional)
  Client secret.

* `password` -
  (Optional)
  Password.

* `token_endpoint` -
  (Optional)
  Token endpoint to get the OAuth token from.

* `user` -
  (Optional)
  Username.

<a name="nested_details_sentinelone_alert_settings"></a>The `sentinelone_alert_settings` block supports:

  HTTP header based authentication.

* `hostname` -

* `initial_start_time` -

* `is_alert_api_subscribed` -
  (Optional)
  Is the customer subscribed to Alerts Api

* `header_key_values` -
  (Optional)
  Header key-value pairs.

* `feedname` -

* `hostname` -
  (Optional)

<a name="nested_details_service_now_cmdb_settings_authentication"></a>The `authentication` block supports:

<a name="nested_details_sftp_settings"></a>The `sftp_settings` block supports:

* `authentication` -
  (Optional)
  SFTP Auth.
  Structure is [documented below](#nested_details_sftp_settings_authentication).

* `source_deletion_option` -
  (Optional)
  Possible values:
  SOURCE_DELETION_NEVER
  SOURCE_DELETION_ON_SUCCESS
  SOURCE_DELETION_ON_SUCCESS_FILES_ONLY

* `source_type` -
  (Optional)
  Possible values:
  FILES
  FOLDERS
  FOLDERS_RECURSIVE

* `uri` -
  (Optional)
  SFTP URI.


<a name="nested_details_sftp_settings_authentication"></a>The `authentication` block supports:

* `password` -
  (Optional)
  Password. Used for username and password authentication.
  **Note**: This property is sensitive and will not be displayed in the plan.

* `private_key` -
  (Optional)
  Private key. Used for private key authentication.
  **Note**: This property is sensitive and will not be displayed in the plan.

* `private_key_passphrase` -
  (Optional)
  Private key passphrase. Used for private key authentication.

* `username` -
  (Optional)
  Username. Used for username and password authentication.

<a name="nested_details_trellix_hx_alerts_settings"></a>The `trellix_hx_alerts_settings` block supports:

* `authentication` -
  (Optional)
  TrellixStarXAuthentication contains a oneof with all of the authentication
  types supported by Trellix *X devices.
  Structure is [documented below](#nested_details_trellix_hx_alerts_settings_authentication).

* `endpoint` -
  (Optional)
  Trellix HX Device URL.
  This must be a valid URL with an http or https scheme. It has no default.
  Usually a device URL is in the form of either:
  https://xxx.trellix.com/hx/id//
  - or -
  https://htapdeviceproxy.md.mandiant.net/dphb/hx//


<a name="nested_details_trellix_hx_alerts_settings_authentication"></a>The `authentication` block supports:

* `msso` -
  (Optional)
  Info for MssoAuthentication using a username, password, and login api
  endpoint.
  Structure is [documented below](#nested_details_trellix_hx_alerts_settings_authentication_msso).

* `trellix_iam` -
  (Optional)
  Settings for TrellixIAMAuthentication.
  Structure is [documented below](#nested_details_trellix_hx_alerts_settings_authentication_trellix_iam).


<a name="nested_details_trellix_hx_alerts_settings_authentication_msso"></a>The `msso` block supports:

* `api_endpoint` -
  (Optional)
  The login api endpoint url.
  This must be a valid URL with an http or https scheme. It has no default.

* `password` -
  (Optional)
  Password of the account identified by username.
  There are no restrictions on the format of the password. It has no default,
  specifically enforced min / max length or character set. The password
  will have been provided by an MSSO administrator and it is assumed that
  they have provided a password that is internally consistent with MSSO
  authentication requirements / validation.
  **Note**: This property is sensitive and will not be displayed in the plan.

* `username` -
  (Optional)
  Username for MSSO authentication.
  There are no restrictions on the format of the username. It has no default,
  specifically enforced min / max length or character set. The username
  will have been provided by an MSSO administrator and it is assumed that
  they have provided a username that is internally consistent with MSSO
  authentication requirements / validation.

<a name="nested_details_trellix_hx_alerts_settings_authentication_trellix_iam"></a>The `trellix_iam` block supports:

* `client_id` -
  (Optional)
  Client ID generated in Trellix IAM.
  This is a unique identifier for the user that is generated in Trellix IAM.
  It has no default, specifically enforced min / max length or character set.
  It is assumed that the Client ID generated in Trellix IAM is internally
  consistent with Trellix IAM authentication requirements / validation.

* `client_secret` -
  (Optional)
  Secret associated with the Client ID.
  This is the secret generated in Trellix IAM for the Client ID. It has no
  default, specifically enforced min / max length or character set. It is
  assumed that the secret generated in Trellix IAM is internally
  consistent with Trellix IAM authentication requirements / validation.
  **Note**: This property is sensitive and will not be displayed in the plan.

* `scope` -
  (Optional)
  OAUTH 2 scope to request for the authentication token.
  This is the OAUTH 2 scope to request for the authentication token. It has
  no default, specifically enforced min / max length or character set. It is
  assumed that the scope provided is internally consistent with Trellix IAM
  authentication requirements / validation.

<a name="nested_details_trellix_hx_bulk_acqs_settings"></a>The `trellix_hx_bulk_acqs_settings` block supports:

* `authentication` -
  (Optional)
  TrellixStarXAuthentication contains a oneof with all of the authentication
  types supported by Trellix *X devices.
  Structure is [documented below](#nested_details_trellix_hx_bulk_acqs_settings_authentication).

* `endpoint` -
  (Required)
  Trellix HX Device URL.
  This must be a valid URL with an http or https scheme. It has no default.
  Usually a device URL is in the form of either:
  https://xxx.trellix.com/hx/id//
  - or -
  https://htapdeviceproxy.md.mandiant.net/dphb/hx//

  endpoint.
  Structure is [documented below](#nested_details_trellix_hx_bulk_acqs_settings_authentication_msso).

* `trellix_iam` -
  (Optional)
  Settings for TrellixIAMAuthentication.
  Structure is [documented below](#nested_details_trellix_hx_bulk_acqs_settings_authentication_trellix_iam).

<a name="nested_details_workday_settings"></a>The `workday_settings` block supports:

* `authentication` -
  (Optional)
  Authentication for Workday.
  Structure is [documented below](#nested_details_workday_settings_authentication).

* `hostname` -
  (Optional)
  API Hostname.

* `tenant_id` -
  (Optional)
  Tenant ID.


<a name="nested_details_workday_settings_authentication"></a>The `authentication` block supports:

* `client_id` -
  (Optional)
  Client ID.

* `client_secret` -
  (Optional)
  Client Secret.
  **Note**: This property is sensitive and will not be displayed in the plan.

* `refresh_token` -
  (Optional)
  Refresh Token.

* `secret` -
  (Optional)
  The access token used to authenticate against Workday. This field is called
  "secret" to maintain backwards compatibility. Workday was (only) configured
  using username (which was unused) and secret (which is used as the access
  token). Either this field or all of the other OAuth fields below must be
  specified.
  **Note**: This property is sensitive and will not be displayed in the plan.

* `token_endpoint` -
  (Optional)
  Token endpoint to get the OAuth token from.

* `user` -
  (Optional)
  Username. This is unused: Workday feeds were originally configured using a
  username and secret authentication method, but only the secret field was
  used, and it was used to supply the OAuth access token.

  Issuer. Usually the client_id.

* `subject` -
  (Optional)
  Subject. Usually the email.

<a name="nested_details_workspace_groups_settings_authentication_rs_credentials"></a>The `rs_credentials` block supports:

* `private_key` -
  (Optional)
  Private key in PEM format.
  **Note**: This property is sensitive and will not be displayed in the plan.

<a name="nested_details_workspace_mobile_settings"></a>The `workspace_mobile_settings` block supports:

  Structure is [documented below](#nested_details_workspace_mobile_settings_authentication).

* `workspace_customer_id` -
  (Optional)
  Customer ID.


<a name="nested_details_workspace_mobile_settings_authentication"></a>The `authentication` block supports:

  (Optional)

  (Optional)
  RS credentials.
  Structure is [documented below](#nested_details_workspace_mobile_settings_authentication_rs_credentials).

  (Optional)
  Token endpoint to get the OAuth token from.

<a name="nested_failure_details"></a>The `failure_details` block supports:

* `error_action` -
  (Output)
  error_action contains the user action prescribed for remediation of feed
  error.

* `error_cause` -
  (Output)
  error_cause contains the information regarding the failure cause.

* `error_code` -
  (Output)
  error_code contains the error code for the feed. The field is populated for
  the feeds with failed status.

* `http_error_code` -
  (Output)
  http_error_code contains the HTTP error code for the feed failure.
  feed transfer failure may or may not result in http error code.

## Attributes Reference

In addition to the arguments listed above, the following computed attributes are exported:

* `id` - an identifier for the resource with format `projects/{{project}}/locations/{{location}}/instances/{{instance}}/feeds/{{feed}}`

* `state` -
  The state of the feed (e.g., ACTIVE, INACTIVE).

* `failure_msg` -
  Details about the most recent failure when feed state is FAILED.

* `last_feed_initiation_time` -
  Latest timestamp when the transfer was successful for the feed.

* `name` -
  The resource name of the feed.
  Format:
  projects/{project}/locations/{location}/instances/{instance}/feeds/{feed}

* `read_only` -
  Whether this feed can be updated or deleted.

* `reference_id` -
  Reference ID, this field will contain the legacy id of the feed.

* `uid` -
  Unique identifier for the feed.


## Timeouts

This resource provides the following
[Timeouts](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/plugin/sdkv2/resources/retries-and-customizable-timeouts) configuration options:

- `create` - Default is 20 minutes.
- `update` - Default is 20 minutes.
- `delete` - Default is 20 minutes.

## Import


Feed can be imported using any of these accepted formats:

* `projects/{{project}}/locations/{{location}}/instances/{{instance}}/feeds/{{feed}}`
* `{{project}}/{{location}}/{{instance}}/{{feed}}`
* `{{location}}/{{instance}}/{{feed}}`


In Terraform v1.5.0 and later, use an [`import` block](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/language/import) to import Feed using one of the formats above. For example:

```tf
import {
  id = "projects/{{project}}/locations/{{location}}/instances/{{instance}}/feeds/{{feed}}"
  to = google_chronicle_feed.default
}
```

When using the [`terraform import` command](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/cli/commands/import), Feed can be imported using one of the formats above. For example:

```
$ terraform import google_chronicle_feed.default projects/{{project}}/locations/{{location}}/instances/{{instance}}/feeds/{{feed}}
$ terraform import google_chronicle_feed.default {{project}}/{{location}}/{{instance}}/{{feed}}
$ terraform import google_chronicle_feed.default {{location}}/{{instance}}/{{feed}}
```

## User Project Overrides

This resource supports [User Project Overrides](https://registry.terraform.io/providers/hashicorp/google/latest/docs/guides/provider_reference#user_project_override).
